title: Deaths due to unsafe sex
subtitle: Estimated annual number of deaths attributed to [unsafe sex](#dod:unsafe_sex). Unsafe sex is defined as the risk of disease due to sexual transmission. Unsafe sex is an important risk factor in cervical cancer, HIV and sexually-transmitted infections.
caption:
Deaths due to unsafe sex

Estimated annual number of deaths attributed to unsafe sex. Unsafe sex is defined as the risk of disease due to sexual transmission. Unsafe sex is an important risk factor in cervical cancer, HIV and sexually-transmitted infections.

The choropleth map displays the estimated annual number of deaths attributed to unsafe sex for the year 2023, utilizing a color scale ranging from light beige for zero deaths to dark maroon for 300,000 deaths. The highest mortality rates, represented by the darkest red shades indicating values between 100,000 and 300,000, are concentrated in Southern Africa, specifically South Africa, as well as in India. A secondary cluster of elevated deaths, falling within the 30,000 to 100,000 range, is visible across much of East Africa, including Tanzania and Kenya, and extends to China and Russia. In the Americas, the United States and Brazil appear in the medium orange band, corresponding to a range of 10,000 to 30,000 deaths. Most of Western Europe, Australia, and Canada are shaded in the lightest beige tones, signifying death counts between 0 and 1,000. Greenland and several nations in the Middle East and Central Africa are marked with diagonal hatching to indicate that no data is available for those regions. The visual distribution highlights a distinct geographic disparity, with the most intense coloration focused heavily on the African continent and parts of South Asia, while large portions of the Northern Hemisphere and Oceania display significantly lower figures.
